title: boehmeria virgata subsp. macrophylla
intro: Boehmeria virgata subsp. macrophylla, also known as the largeleaf nettle tree or giant Boehmeria, is a plant species of the family Urticaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Boehmeria virgata subsp. macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make infusions, teas, and poultices, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken orally to reap the plant's medicinal benefits.
